shared_concepts: llm:Both involve constant acceleration on inclined planes; OSK-2003-04 establishes basic kinematics before adding friction in OSK-2003-05.
llm:OSK-2003-05 introduces friction on a single block; OSK-2003-06 extends this to a multi-body system with friction and pulleys.
llm:OSP-2002-04 introduces basic relative motion; OSK-2005-07 applies relative motion and variable mass concepts to a sand timer system.
llm:OSK-2004-01 establishes orbital mechanics basics; OSK-2005-09 applies relative motion and gravity concepts to gravity assist maneuvers.
llm:OSK-2004-02 covers vertical circular motion dynamics; OSN-2004-01 applies conservation of energy and dynamics to sliding off a curved track.
llm:OSP-2003-04 deals with 1D/2D collisions on smooth tracks; OSN-2004-02 advances to 2D inelastic collisions with specific geometric constraints.
llm:OSK-2005-08 covers collision with a pivoted rod; OSN-2004-05 extends this to elastic collision with a free rod requiring angular momentum conservation.
llm:Coulomb's law and electric field basics are prerequisites for understanding electrostatic effects in capacitors
llm:Magnetic force on charges is a fundamental concept for magnetron operation
llm:Basic electric field and charge concepts are needed for parallel plate capacitor analysis
llm:Coulomb's law and electric field concepts are foundational for detecting conductive objects
llm:Basic capacitor and circuit concepts are needed for non-linear circuit analysis
llm:Specific heat and temperature concepts in OSN-2018-04 are foundational for ideal gas law and specific heat applications in OSN-2024-04.
llm:Basic capacitor theory is required to understand temperature-sensitive capacitor circuits
llm:OSK-2003-03 covers projectile motion with constant forces; OSP-2003-01 applies projectile concepts to elastic collisions with inclined planes.
llm:OSK-2003-02 handles relative motion in projectile contexts; OSP-2004-01 combines relative motion with conservation laws on a moving platform.
